title: Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio
summary: Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio retrieves prior skills in an original independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css transitions, css keyframes, css animation timing, css animation performance, css reduced motion, css animation debug in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Ethical Product Launch motion and feedback work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the Ethical Product Launch motion and feedback work here. */

steps.title: 01 · portfolio task and attention audit inspect 1 · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Audit a professional portfolio whose navigation underline, project-card reveal, and submission confirmation compete for attention across keyboard and touch use. Which conclusion is justified by keyboard focus continuity, rapid reversal behavior, a no-motion task path, touch-target feedback, and stakeholder task timing? Scenario: independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer.
steps.why: Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer.

content.title: independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer evidence
content.text: For Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio, choose the css transitions claim that preserves the independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 1: transition all is harmless because browsers automatically choose only cheap useful properties. Treat the portfolio task and attention audit appearance as sufficient proof.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 1: name the exact state change and properties, keep both endpoints understandable, and verify interruption, keyboard, preference, and performance behavior. Apply that contract to portfolio task and attention audit and retain changed-case evidence.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 1: delay button activation until its hover transition finishes so users see the complete effect. Skip keyboard focus continuity, rapid reversal behavior, a no-motion task path, touch-target feedback, and stakeholder task timing.

stimulus.title: portfolio task and attention audit trace 1
stimulus.caption: Evidence from Audit a professional portfolio whose navigation underline, project-card reveal, and submission confirmation compete for attention across keyboard and touch use.

lines.label: observed risk
lines.text: Transition all is harmless because browsers automatically choose only cheap useful properties.

lines.label: acceptance evidence
lines.text: keyboard focus continuity, rapid reversal behavior, a no-motion task path, touch-target feedback, and stakeholder task timing

steps.title: 03 · portfolio task and attention audit answer 3 · lab

content.title: independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer evidence
content.text: For Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio, choose the css animation timing claim that preserves the independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio checkpoint 3: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio checkpoint 3: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Lab: Add Purposeful Motion to a Professional Portfolio checkpoint 3: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 3: longer bouncier animation always makes an interface more engaging. Treat the portfolio task and attention audit appearance as sufficient proof.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 3: choose every timeline control from feedback purpose and user control, then verify rapid input, interruption, completion, and attention cost. Apply that contract to portfolio task and attention audit and retain changed-case evidence.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 3: use animation delay to postpone the actual form submission until the illustration completes. Skip keyboard focus continuity, rapid reversal behavior, a no-motion task path, touch-target feedback, and stakeholder task timing.

options.text: Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 4: transform and opacity are always free and need no performance verification. Treat the portfolio task and attention audit appearance as sufficient proof.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 4: prefer transform or opacity when suitable, measure actual layout paint compositing and memory behavior, and remove speculative promotion hints. Apply that contract to portfolio task and attention audit and retain changed-case evidence.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 4: replace a measured cheap color change with a large composited layer because compositing is always faster. Skip keyboard focus continuity, rapid reversal behavior, a no-motion task path, touch-target feedback, and stakeholder task timing.

options.text: Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 11: a very fast animation automatically satisfies reduced-motion needs. Treat the portfolio task and attention audit appearance as sufficient proof.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 11: remove nonessential spatial movement while preserving status and task feedback through immediate nonmotion cues and user control. Apply that contract to portfolio task and attention audit and retain changed-case evidence.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 11: run the full orbit in one millisecond so it technically spends less time moving. Skip keyboard focus continuity, rapid reversal behavior, a no-motion task path, touch-target feedback, and stakeholder task timing.

options.text: Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 20: keyframe percentages are elapsed milliseconds in the animation timeline. Treat the portfolio task and attention audit appearance as sufficient proof.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 20: define purposeful start intermediate and end states as relative progress, then preserve a stable nonanimated status outside the sequence. Apply that contract to portfolio task and attention audit and retain changed-case evidence.

Independent transition keyframe reduced-motion and performance transfer case 20: use fill-mode both as the only way the completed status remains visible. Skip keyboard focus continuity, rapid reversal behavior, a no-motion task path, touch-target feedback, and stakeholder task timing.
